title: Warez R.I.P. — OpenCart extensions and themes safety checker
meta-description: Security and reputation analysis service for OpenCart marketplaces distributing modules and themes. Detection of piracy, fraud, and unauthorized content distribution indicators.
search-help: Enter the address of a marketplace with OpenCart themes or extensions
result-known: Note: we do not monitor the content of such websites and cannot guarantee their safety
modal-warez-intro:
Warez (from wares, plural software) refers to illegally distributed software that violates copyright laws, often with modified or cracked source code.

Usually, the code is modified to allow free use, but as a “bonus”, users of pirated extensions (modules and templates) often receive various types of malware — trojans, miners, hidden ads, invisible links, and more. This is not random — scammers aim to monetize their activity.

Websites distributing pirated software often encode their files using the eval() function. You can read about the risks of using this function in the official PHP documentation:
modal-warez-warning-text: Using eval() can be extremely dangerous as it allows execution of arbitrary code. Its usage is strongly discouraged. If you are absolutely sure there is no alternative way to implement the required functionality, pay special attention to data validation and exception handling.
modal-warez-consequences-title: What are the consequences of using warez?
modal-warez-consequences-intro: As the saying goes, nothing comes for free — using pirated extensions usually leads to the following consequences:
modal-warez-consequence-1-text: Unknown code, files, or links may appear on your website without your knowledge
modal-warez-consequence-2-text: Scammers may use your store to promote their own websites without your consent
modal-warez-consequence-3-text: You won’t be able to contact the original developer for support — doing so may result in being blocked
modal-warez-consequence-4-text: Malicious code may be used to send spam through your store’s mail server
modal-warez-consequence-5-text: Security vulnerabilities make your website much easier to hack, and the breach may go unnoticed
modal-warez-primary-signs-intro: Key indicators that help detect fraudulent websites:
modal-warez-primary-sign-1-title: Check the website name on Google
modal-warez-primary-sign-1-text: Often, information about piracy or stolen extensions appears already on the first search page
modal-warez-primary-sign-2-text: It usually contains generic phrases like ‘a team of experienced developers’ or ‘many years of experience with OpenCart’ without real details or portfolio
modal-warez-primary-sign-3-title: Look for the author of a specific module or template
modal-warez-primary-sign-3-text: Pirate websites may offer hundreds of extensions, but they usually have a single author or none at all
modal-warez-primary-sign-4-title: If an author is listed, check their name or nickname on Google
modal-warez-primary-sign-4-text: If nothing appears, the author is likely fake — otherwise you would find profiles on legitimate marketplaces or personal websites
modal-warez-primary-sign-5-title: Check for demo sites and their domains
modal-warez-primary-sign-5-text: Most extensions will not have demos, although sometimes links to real official demos are added for appearance
modal-warez-primary-sign-6-title: Check for paid ‘VIP accounts’, subscriptions, etc.
modal-warez-primary-sign-6-text: Mechanisms granting access to all extensions from different authors are not possible on legitimate marketplaces
modal-warez-primary-sign-7-text: Pirate websites never provide real legal details — contacts usually include only email or Telegram
modal-warez-secondary-signs-title: What other signs of piracy exist?
modal-warez-secondary-signs-intro: Illegal resources often show even more obvious indicators:
modal-warez-secondary-sign-1: Group-buying (‘warez pools’) are organized for single extensions
modal-warez-secondary-sign-2: You may see terms like ‘nulled’, ‘cracked’, ‘leak’, ‘ionCube-free’ in listings
modal-warez-secondary-sign-3: You are required to perform actions before downloading (like, subscribe, etc.)
modal-warez-secondary-sign-4: Downloads are restricted for new users but can be unlocked for a fee
modal-warez-secondary-sign-5: Download links are hidden under spoilers and require payment to reveal
modal-warez-secondary-sign-6: Downloaded files are password-protected and the password must be purchased
modal-warez-secondary-sign-7: The site copies design or elements from official websites
modal-warez-secondary-sign-8: Download links point to third-party file hosts instead of the site’s own server
modal-warez-secondary-signs-outro: Of course, these are not all indicators of a dangerous website. If you are unsure, use this verification tool and enter the suspicious URL. If we have any information about it, you will receive it immediately.
modal-trusted-intro: Distinguishing trusted websites from illegal ones that distribute cracked and malicious software under the guise of legitimate products is not particularly difficult in practice. The number of trustworthy marketplaces is limited, and their list is well known. In Eastern Europe and neighboring regions, there are 6 most well-known OpenCart marketplaces:
modal-trusted-site-1-text: The largest thematic forum with an extensions store and the popular ocStore build
modal-trusted-site-2-text: A well-known extensions marketplace operating since 2014
modal-trusted-site-3-text: An extensions marketplace with its own custom build
modal-trusted-site-4-text: A project specialized in video reviews
modal-trusted-site-5-text: A forum-based project with an extensions marketplace
modal-trusted-site-6-text: A new marketplace from extension developers
modal-trusted-outro:
There is also the official OpenCart website and the international marketplace, but this overview focuses primarily on the local market, where such violations are more common.

Personal websites and extension stores of individual authors are not included, as there are many of them and they are harder to track. Their authenticity can usually be verified directly by contacting the author.
modal-trusted-attention-text: If a website is not a personal developer project and is not part of the well-known marketplaces listed above, it should be treated with increased caution and verified before use.
modal-about-text:
If you are visiting this page for the first time, you may wonder who determines whether a marketplace is trustworthy and on what basis?

Many members of the OpenCart community have long known me under the nickname RGB. This non-commercial project was created as a simple verification tool and is maintained through personal experience and contributions from community members. Its purpose is to help users and developers identify websites distributing pirated OpenCart modules and themes.

The service has been publicly available since May 2018, as confirmed by the forum discussion. Over the years, it has become a well-known tool within the community and is regularly referenced in the context of checking suspicious resources.

Websites are added to the database based on a combination of indicators rather than isolated incidents. All resources undergo thorough review before inclusion, and domain history is also taken into account. Therefore, changing a website’s topic or activity after being added to the database does not affect the evaluation of its previous history. Over the entire lifetime of the service, cases of decision review have been extremely rare, making accidental errors unlikely.

There have also been attempts by pirate websites to copy or partially reproduce the service. If you come across a similar project using a domain other than warez.rip and featuring a different list of websites, it is recommended to verify its authenticity carefully.
modal-about-contacts-text: Contact us if you have any questions or would like to share information
modal-contributors-text:
I would like to express my sincere gratitude to everyone who has helped and continues to help with building the database of pirated websites and developing the project’s ideas. Special thanks go to spectre and AlexDW. Thank you!

I also thank all other community members who contributed to the development of the service:
modal-disclaimer-text:
The service is informational and analytical in nature and provides independent expert evaluation of websites.

The presented assessment is based on analysis of available information and reflects a combined result of data processing.

The service neither confirms nor denies violations of applicable laws and does not determine the legal status of any resources.

The decision to interact with any third-party websites is made solely by the user.

The service may use cookies and analytics technologies to collect anonymized visit statistics and improve website functionality.
modal-methodology-text:
Website evaluation is based on the analysis of a combination of signals.

Data about the website from search engines and related resources is taken into account, including mentions, reviews, and information about possible violations or distribution of unauthorized content.

Information about the website owner is analyzed, including the presence of contact and legal details and the level of transparency of the provided information.

The structure of the website and its content are evaluated, including the nature of materials and how they are organized.

Content distribution methods are taken into account, including the use of third-party services, external download sources, access restrictions, and paid file access mechanisms.

Indirect indicators that may suggest potentially unauthorized content distribution are also considered, including mass publication of materials, hidden authorship, subscription-based access models, and download restrictions.

Signs of design or structure copying from known resources are also taken into account.

Additionally, a comparison is performed with known legitimate and illegitimate sources, as well as previously identified cases of violations.
modal-report-alert-text: We currently have no information about this website
modal-report-main-text: Let us know the website address and what raised concerns about its legitimacy
modal-report-text-1: Report fraudulent websites and malicious software
modal-report-text-2: Report violations on websites using the Cloudflare service
modal-report-text-3: Report suspicious content that violates rules or laws
modal-report-outro: If you believe the website poses a threat, using the services listed above may help initiate additional action against violators.
footer-brand-text: OpenCart marketplaces safety and reputation checker based on multi-factor risk analysis
